messages.content: Always use code blocks with the appropriate language tags. If asked for a table always format it using Markdown syntax.

The `git push --force-with-lease` command is used to forcefully update the remote branch with your local branch while also respecting the commit history of the remote branch. It is a safer alternative to `git push --force`, which forcibly updates the remote branch without considering any changes that may have been made to the remote branch by others.

When you use `git push --force-with-lease`, Git verifies that the remote branch's tip is still at the same commit as your local branch's tip. If it is, then Git will update the remote branch. However, if the remote branch's tip has been updated by others, Git will reject the push, preventing you from accidentally overwriting someone else's work.

This command is useful when you want to update a remote branch with your changes, but you want to ensure that you are not unintentionally overwriting changes made by others since you last pulled from the remote repository.
